cb65425b-cc3d-4f8a-8164-5849f594d708.title: Build data by onboarding 5 additional customers
cb65425b-cc3d-4f8a-8164-5849f594d708.exec_summary: Goal: onboard the next 5 hand-picked customers and run AP & AR reconciliation on neno, with onboarding calls targeted for 6-7 July (now upon us - the 6 Jul target date has passed). Open banking is now TESTABLE: Dima is plugging Yapily into the onboarding actions, and the decision is to hide the customer transaction view initially (messy data) and show account name + balance only, with consent stored so transactions can be re-pulled later. Multi-Client Exact Online Connections shipped and closed on 2 Jul (per-workspace connections, broken-connection detection, fail-closed accountant UI). Non-Swan feature re-provisioning continues (Joel): Vault (TC3) shipped, email-forwarding inbox (TC2) in progress, TC4 Money Management remains. Contract signing now auto-creates a fresh empty workspace for non-Swan customers, and the team is scoping Letter-of-Engagement automation (DocuSign or an EU open-source signing tool; PandaDocs ruled out). Main worries: the onboarding-actions project is still Planned while calls are happening, the bankTransaction.reference schema change is needed before OB ingestion is reliable, the WhatsApp/Meta Business setup is misconfigured, and the Exact 60 req/min rate limit is already being hit.
whats_changed: Open banking is now testable (7 Jul): Yapily is being wired into the onboarding actions (Dima); the customer transaction view will be hidden initially - account name and balance only - with consent captured in the DB so transactions can be re-pulled later.

Non-Swan workspaces: contract signing auto-creates a fresh empty workspace (no Swan link); cashback onboarding screens done for Swan users with a new card-order prompt during onboarding (mobile-app screens and homepage cashback summary still outstanding); the OB-to-Swan cashback upsell idea is parked.

Letter-of-Engagement automation direction chosen (6 Jul): one button generating pre-filled letters per entity with signing tracked into HubSpot; PandaDocs ruled out on cost, likely DocuSign or an EU open-source signing tool; HubSpot deal context to be surfaced in the accounting-engagement screen.

Multi-Client Exact Online Connections shipped and marked Completed (2 Jul): per-workspace Exact connections managed by Ops, dead-connection detection + #tech-critical alerts, accountant UI fails closed (PRs #1102/1107/1116/1131/1133).

Open Banking real-bank testing (1 Jul): a live ABN AMRO account immediately hit the bankTransaction.reference VARCHAR(255) limit - OB references can reach ~280k chars, so a schema change is needed before ingestion is reliable.
Non-Swan feature re-provisioning progressing: Vault uploads (TC3, NEO-1360) shipped; the email-forwarding inbox (TC2, NEO-1359) in progress; TC4 Money Management remains.

Board curation (7 Jul): 'Enable team members outside Swan' descoped for July - the first five are single users, manual user-add is the quick fix; replaced by 'Enable invoicing for non-Swan customers' (priority: IBAN selection for open-banking customers).

risks.text: Onboarding calls (6-7 Jul) are here but the onboarding-actions project is still Planned; its stepper dependency is softened by the decision to hide the transaction view initially, but the guided flow is not shipped.

Open-banking ingestion needs a bankTransaction.reference schema change: the current VARCHAR(255) truncates/errors on real ABN AMRO data (OB references can reach ~280k chars); large-TEXT vs lookup-performance trade-off to be agreed with Mark before a PR.
Exact API rate limit (60 req/min) already hit in testing with seeded data; needs a roadmap solution (connection pool or backpressure).
WhatsApp/Meta Business account setup is misconfigured and was deprioritised (24 Jun); blocks the WhatsApp bill channel rollout.
Email forwarding only accepts workspace-member senders, has no UI yet, and the rejected-email auto-reply is still to be built.

projects.name: Connect external bank accounts (Open Banking)
projects.summary: Provider-agnostic transaction/balance ingestion plus a multi-account Money Management UI rework. Now testable; Yapily being wired into onboarding actions; transaction view hidden initially (account name + balance only).
blockers: bankTransaction.reference schema change needed before reliable ingestion (real ABN AMRO data).

projects.name: Feature re-provisioning for non Swan customers
projects.summary: Make WhatsApp, email forwarding, Vault and Money Management work for non-Swan customers. Vault (TC3) shipped; email-forwarding inbox (TC2) in progress; TC4 Money Management remains.

projects.name: Multi-Client Exact Online Connections
projects.summary: Shipped 2 Jul: per-workspace Exact connections managed by Ops, each workspace reads/writes its own division, OAuth grants separated from assignments, dead-connection detection + alerts, fail-closed accountant UI.

projects.name: Onboarding Actions for the Next Five Customers
projects.summary: Guided Home onboarding hub: connect bank (invokes open banking), learn the three bill channels, a guided sample task; Home hides when done.
blockers: Still Planned while the 6-7 Jul onboarding calls happen; open-banking connect flow is the invoked dependency.

projects.name: Additional changes for go-live
projects.summary: Miscellaneous changes needed to get onboarding customers fully go-live ready.

decisions.text: The customer transaction view is hidden initially for open-banking accounts (data can be messy) - show account name and balance only; consent captured in the DB allows re-pulling transactions later. Open banking is now testable, with Dima plugging Yapily into the onboarding actions.
decisions.source: Granola - 3 amigos: Walk through customer onboarding, 7 Jul

decisions.text: Non-Swan workspaces: contract signing auto-creates a fresh empty workspace (no Swan link). Cashback: card-order prompt added to Swan onboarding; cashback prompt removed from the account page; OB-to-Swan cashback upsell parked.

Letter-of-Engagement automation: one button triggers pre-filled letters per entity with signing tracked back into HubSpot; PandaDocs ruled out (cost), Google Workspace has no signature API - likely DocuSign or an EU open-source signing tool. HubSpot deal context to be surfaced in the neno accounting-engagement screen.

'Enable team members outside Swan' descoped for July: the first five customers are single users only, with manual user-add as the quick fix; replaced on the board by 'Enable invoicing for non-Swan customers' (priority: IBAN selection for open-banking customers).

Multi-Client Exact Online Connections is complete: Ops manage per-workspace Exact connections; each workspace reads/writes its own division; OAuth grants separated from assignments; broken connections detected, shown in the Ops dashboard and alerted to #tech-critical; accountant UI fails closed.

Open Banking is being tested end-to-end against a real ABN AMRO account; the bankTransaction.reference column (VARCHAR(255)) is too short for real OB data (references can reach ~280k chars), so a schema change is required before ingestion is reliable.

Re-provisioning project renamed to 'Feature re-provisioning for non Swan customers' and broadened; added TC4 (Money Management): Transactions populated from open-banking-connected accounts (empty if none), Accounts and Invoicing visible and functional.

Onboarding uses magic-link sign-on and a self-marked actions checklist reusing existing pages; completed tasks hide and the homepage is removed from nav once done; the accountant profile block is copied to the tasks screen. Dima + Eugenia own the build.
decisions.source: Granola - Onboarding Actions with Dima / Three amigos, 25 Jun

decisions.text: Page visibility for open-banking customers: show Account, Transactions and Invoices; Transactions appears once a bank is connected and transactions exist; Bills & expenses is .build-only and out of scope.
decisions.source: Granola - Onboarding Actions with Dima, 25 Jun

decisions.text: Bank-connect uses the Yapily one-button hand-off model; the homepage shows connected accounts and prompts for more; WhatsApp setup collapses once connected. Dummy onboarding task marked as a sample; all three receipt channels must be explored before it is done.
First open-banking target customer is As Controla (no Swan account); provider selected is Montoya; onboarding calls targeted for 6-7 July.
Onboarding is a fixed 6-step, self-reported checklist; both the transactions drawer and the tasks/upload drawer expose all three bill-submission channels (WhatsApp, email forwarding, Vault).

open_questions.text: Onboarding stepper remains a placeholder until the new transactions UI is ready (dependency softened by hiding the transaction view initially).
How does Exact count rate limits - per token or per division? Determines whether a connection pool actually helps.
Onboarding V2 deprecation date conflict: Swan email says 26 Jun, live docs say 30 Sep - Cecile to confirm.
Receipt upload drawer is missing WhatsApp + email options (flagged 22 Jun).
Accountant assignment for new workspaces: add to the accounting-engagements page (Yaroslav's) or hard-code for the initial five?

next_steps.text: Document the customer-onboarding user journey as-is; share the updated task-screen prototype + missing-context summary in Slack for Dima.
Review onboarding screens for open-banking customers (incl. the cashback prompt); prioritise Q3 bookkeeping features for the first five.
Prototype a HubSpot agent in the existing chat interface; book time with Euge to walk the user journey.
Fix the bankTransaction.reference schema so OB references (up to ~280k chars) store without truncation; agree the trade-off with Mark before the PR.
Fix the Transactions page visibility/permissions for open-banking customers.
Test the magic-link onboarding flow end-to-end in a new workspace.

fef38f90-b82c-4c47-b377-6dcb9b1a3020.title: Enable accountants to complete VAT submissions via double-entry GL

notes.text: Full specification for this build lives in the native-GL knowledgebase (an Obsidian vault) on PR #1128: the user-observable outcomes in 'General Ledger - Truth Conditions' (TC-LEDGER-01..15), the system rules in 'General Ledger - Invariants' (INV-LEDGER-01..22), and the architecture narrative in the 'General Ledger' domain hub. In-scope-now items (TC-LEDGER-01..06 / INV-LEDGER-01..13) read 'Holds'; deferred future-build items (TC-LEDGER-07..15 / INV-LEDGER-14..22) read 'Proposed (deferred)'.

The knowledgebase is a behavioural 'digital twin': Truth Condition -> Invariant -> Scenario -> test -> code, traceable by id (TC-/INV-/SCN-LEDGER-NN). Tests cite the scenario id they prove; code cites the invariant id it satisfies. Reading order is top-down from the domain hub.
Agentic-ledger idea: when a backdated entry is posted, automatically detect which reports are affected and surface the delta.

fef38f90-b82c-4c47-b377-6dcb9b1a3020.exec_summary: Standing up Neno's own double-entry general ledger so accountants can file Q3 VAT for the first cohort from Neno's own data. The native GL (NEO-1361) is now implemented and covered by green tests on the build branch, gated behind an off-by-default feature flag pending a deliberate production go-live - superseding the earlier dual-write spike that mirrored Exact's codes. The design inverts the old model: Neno owns the chart of accounts and VAT codes, every accounting action emits an event that a reliable engine books into a balanced, VAT-aware journal entry with counterparty and VAT-code dimensions, and Exact becomes a removable downstream sync. Scope for this build is the VAT return, live/complete cash, append-only auditability and drill-down; period locking, opening balances, multi-currency, the full report set and XBRL filing are specified but deferred.
whats_changed: DE GL Decisions session (6 Jul, Mark/Matthew): settlement gets an AMOUNT check (Decision 08 / INV-30) - the bank-entry leg and bill leg must match on amount before reconciling, not just balance to zero; root cause of the underlying bug was a bill edited after initial booking without re-checking correspondence.
Decision 07 (editing open periods) scoped: break-the-glass covers deliberate edits DURING an open period only; out-of-period corrections are a separate concept - adjustments.
INV-11 confirmed (settled 29 Jun - 2 Jul): no GL account per counterparty; a payables GL account carries a counterparty property, keeping one balance sliceable by counterparty.

VAT recognition clarified with Venla (7 Jul): when an invoice's expense is split across periods, the FULL VAT books to the balance sheet on the invoice date - VAT is not split monthly with the expense. Follow-up open question from Mark: is VAT recognition comparable across countries?
Mark published the GL decisions doc and asked Matthew + Venla to confirm decisions and acknowledge the open questions (7 Jul).

DP/Mark 'open questions' session (30 Jun) locked the FX model: FX is booked at the ECB daily reference (mid) rate frozen on the line; a settlement rate difference posts to a dedicated realised FX gain/loss account and any separate bank FX fee to bank charges; every line stores native amount + transaction-date rate with the reporting-currency value derived (INV-LEDGER-23/27; INV-01 reworded to balance on reporting-currency values).

VAT-correction model firmed: VAT is always corrected forward (closed books never reopened); the chart needs two VAT balance-sheet accounts (normal + corrections/control) and, once the EUR 1,000 cumulative threshold is crossed, all subsequent corrections route to the corrections account until the suppletie is filed (INV-LEDGER-19).

Locked-period corrections gated by a break-the-glass unlock (deliberate, per-workspace approver, full audit log, amount-threshold alerting); FX revaluation deferred but modelled (balance-sheet accounts only, period-end rate, true-up to FX result, USD bank first, on demand).

Status check (Mark, 26 Jun): the design is locked and a first implementation is drafted with green unit tests, but it has NOT yet been run against real production data - the next step is validating the engine on real prod data in dev, which may surface code or architecture changes.

Native double-entry GL (NEO-1361) is built and green-tested on the build branch, behind an off-by-default feature flag; the 'ledger' event destination is now a supported, booked destination (PR #1128).

The work is now specified as a behavioural 'digital twin' knowledgebase (Truth Conditions -> Invariants -> Scenarios -> tests): TC-LEDGER-01..06 and INV-LEDGER-01..13 are in scope and now 'Hold'; TC-LEDGER-07..15 / INV-LEDGER-14..22 are specified but deferred.
Design supersedes the dual-write spike: Neno owns its chart of accounts and VAT codes; Exact is a removable downstream mapping the books never depend on.

VAT modelled as a first-class dimension - VAT code on both base and tax lines, treatment (domestic / reverse-charge / intra-EU / import / export) drives the posting shape; the VAT return is a code->box report, validated against a real filed Dutch return (Happy Team Apps Q1 2026).
Cash completeness confirmed: every bank transaction is booked on import (bank vs suspense) so the balance is live before reconciliation; reconciliation reclassifies out of suspense.
'Source of truth' deliberately reworded to 'source of truth for VAT filing' to avoid scope creep; euro-only for now with XBRL filing deferred.
Go-live now needs an opening-balance backfill or cutoff booking; year-end roll-forward (TC8) flagged CORE but still needs DP sign-off.

risks.text: Native GL build is drafted with green unit tests behind an off-by-default flag but has not yet been run against real production data; the next gate is real-data validation in dev (may surface code/architecture changes) ahead of the go-live cutover (opening balances + Exact decoupling). (PR #1128 / status update 26 Jun)
Belgium gapless-ledger scope still unconfirmed - whether it constrains day-to-day ledger architecture or only closed-period exports/reporting.
DP's full reporting requirements list (potentially 100+ items) not yet compiled - could expand GL scope.

projects.name: Start writing to neno's double-entry GL
projects.summary: Native double-entry ledger now built and green-tested behind an off-by-default flag (NEO-1361): Neno-owned chart + VAT codes, event-driven balanced VAT-aware postings, append-only reversals, cash completeness, and the VAT return as a report. Exact decoupled to a removable downstream sync.
blockers: Production go-live cutover outstanding: opening-balance backfill or cutoff booking, plus Exact decoupling.
Year-end roll-forward (TC8) needs sign-off with DP before it is in scope.

decisions.text: Settlement amount validation (Decision 08 / INV-30): reconciliation checks amount, not just zero-balance - the bank-entry leg and the bill leg must match on amount before reconciling; correspondence is re-checked when a bill is edited after initial booking.
Decision 07 (editing open periods) covers deliberate 'break the glass' edits made during an open period only; edits outside a period are a separate concept - adjustments.
INV-11 confirmed: no separate GL account per counterparty - a payables GL account with a counterparty property, keeping a single account balance sliceable by counterparty.

VAT recognition: when an invoice's expense is split across periods (e.g. Mar-Apr-May), the full VAT amount books to the balance sheet (VAT reclaimable/payable) on the invoice date - VAT is not split monthly with the expense.

FX rate is booked at the ECB daily reference (mid) rate on the transaction date, frozen on the line; when the settling payment clears at a different rate the residual is a realised FX gain/loss posted to a dedicated FX-difference account, and a separate bank FX fee books to bank charges.
decisions.source: Granola - DP/Mark open questions neno GL, 30 Jun / Slack #accounting-mvp, 1 Jul

decisions.text: FX representation: every line stores native amount + transaction-date rate, reporting-currency value derived (INV-LEDGER-23); multi-currency balancing split to INV-LEDGER-27; INV-01 reworded so balance = signed sum of reporting-currency values. FX revaluation (INV-16, deferred): balance-sheet accounts only, revalue to period-end rate, true-up to FX result, USD bank first, on demand.

Locked-period corrections: correct-forward by default; restating a closed period only via a break-the-glass unlock (deliberate, per-workspace approver, full audit log, amount-threshold alerting); prompt when a correction hits an already-filed period.

VAT corrections are always corrected forward; two VAT balance-sheet accounts (normal + corrections/control); once the EUR 1,000 cumulative threshold is crossed all subsequent corrections route to the corrections account until the suppletie is filed. <EUR 1,000 net/year rolls into the next return, >EUR 1,000 goes on a supplementary return.

GL status: design locked and a first implementation drafted with passing unit tests, but not yet run against real production data - next step is feeding real prod data through the engine in dev (may surface code/architecture changes). Still the architecture/spike phase.
decisions.source: Linear project status update, 26 Jun (edited 29 Jun)

decisions.text: Native GL (NEO-1361) is implemented and green-tested behind an off-by-default feature flag; Neno owns the chart and VAT codes and Exact becomes a removable downstream sync - superseding the dual-write spike.

Ledger architecture: every accounting action emits an event; a reliable engine books each into a balanced, VAT-aware journal entry with counterparty and VAT-code dimensions; entries reference their event + source; corrections are append-only contras.
VAT is a first-class dimension: VAT code on base and tax lines; treatment (domestic / reverse-charge / intra-EU / import / export) drives the posting shape; the return maps codes to boxes.
'Source of truth' reworded to 'source of truth for VAT filing' to avoid scope creep from external payment sources.
Book ledger entries when bank transactions import (not only bills/invoices) so the bank balance is knowable before reconciliation (cash completeness via suspense).
Euro-only for now; XBRL statutory filing deferred. Opening balances at go-live handled via a backfill or a cutoff booking.

VAT corrections after a period close are booked in the next period (dated day 1); under EUR 1,000 fold into the next return without a supplemental filing. Balance sheet needs three VAT lines: receivable, payable, corrections/suspense.
Closed periods lock writes by default; unlocking is approval-gated and only for material post-close accruals; insert-only with full audit log.
Insert-only ledger with reversals - no edits to journal entries; corrections reverse and rebook. Period closes define immutability. Monitoring over rollback. Euro-only, multi-currency deferred.

open_questions.text: INV-30: exactly how the reconciliation imbalance occurred mechanically is still unclear (context in the Claude session).
OQ1 party definition: needs a formal write-up with decisions and reasoning (logic exists, no formal doc yet).
INV-23 label: 'multi-currency payments' wording in the summary doc is confusing - foreign lines keep native amount/currency/rate from day one, FX settlement deferred; doc to be reworded.
Is VAT recognition comparable across different countries? (raised by Mark, 8 Jul)

FX balance rule not finally chosen: 'signed sum zero in the entry's one currency' (spike model) vs 'signed sum of reporting-currency values is zero' (native multi-currency); also confirm the full residual account set (FX difference, rounding, payment difference, discount).

Locked-period break-the-glass details unsettled: who approves, what is recorded, and whether the original filing must be resubmitted; plus the audit-log + Slack unlock-alerting design (start by reporting all changes, add amount thresholds once noisy).
Year-end roll-forward (close income/expense into retained earnings; clean new-year P&L + correct opening balance sheet) flagged CORE but still needs DP sign-off.
Belgium gapless-ledger requirement - does it constrain day-to-day ledger architecture or only closed-period exports/reporting?
Full reporting requirements list (potentially 100+ items) still being compiled by DP.
VAT filing period must be configurable per customer/country (monthly, quarterly, or bi-monthly).

next_steps.text: Confirm the decisions and acknowledge the open questions in Mark's GL decisions doc.
Feed real production data through the native GL engine in dev to validate it (expected to surface code/architecture changes).
Decide the FX balance rule (single-currency spike vs native multi-currency) and confirm the full residual account list.
Define the VAT correction/control account in the chart of accounts (separate from the normal VAT account; empty after each quarterly suppletie).
Design the locked-period audit log + Slack unlock alerting (report all changes first, add amount thresholds later).
Finish and send the truth-conditions document.
Confirm TC8 / year-end roll-forward with DP.
Add XBRL to the Slack chat / roadmap.
Review spike code superseded; focus shifts to production go-live (opening balances + Exact decoupling).
Map the VAT export to Nextens for submission.

meetings.title: DE GL Decisions

decisions: Settlement gate checks amount, not just zero-balance (INV-30)
Decision 07 = break-the-glass during open periods; adjustments are separate
INV-11 reconfirmed: counterparty property, not account-per-party

meetings.title: DP / Mark - open questions neno GL

Written up in #accounting-mvp on 1 Jul.
decisions: FX booked at ECB mid rate, residual to realised FX gain/loss, bank fee separate
Native lines carry amount+rate; reporting-currency value derived (INV-23/27/01)
VAT always corrected forward; normal + corrections/control VAT accounts with a EUR 1,000 threshold
open_questions: Single-currency spike vs native multi-currency balance rule; full residual account set
Locked-period break-the-glass approver/record/resubmit + audit-log & alerting design
next_steps: Design locked-period audit log + Slack alerting
Define the VAT correction/control account in the chart of accounts

Book on import for cash completeness

ce07f00e-b392-40bd-85fb-2a4a831c9c80.title: Make neno the source of truth for all bookkeeping

notes.text: Feature set filed as Linear projects (Backlog) with DP-sourced requirements; requirements are updated on each project's summary directly for now, with the source noted.

ce07f00e-b392-40bd-85fb-2a4a831c9c80.exec_summary: The initiative is a filled-out backlog with one committed piece for July: end-to-end AR reconciliation (Planned, currently unstaffed after David's departure on 6 Jul). Depreciation schedules and credit-note processing were deprioritised for July (7 Jul) despite Andries asking for a depreciation schedule from w/c 7 Jul. Meanwhile the live reconciliation workflow is hardening: suggestions are now capped at one per bill and per transaction (NEO-1451, shipped 7 Jul), OI duplicate bank transactions were fixed (NEO-1448, 6 Jul), and a workflow was agreed where Andries reviews the ~55 not-matched transactions and triggers missing-bill tasks for Marloes once the lists are trustworthy. Ihor flagged that the suggestion-dedupe constraint must carry over to AR reconciliation. DP is still compiling the full reporting requirements list (100+ items).
whats_changed: Reconciliation suggestions now limited to one active suggestion per bill and per transaction (NEO-1451, Ihor, shipped 7 Jul); Ihor flagged the same constraint will be needed for AR reconciliation once it exists.

Missing-bill workflow agreed (7 Jul): once the 'Not matched' and 'Needs your review' lists are trustworthy (Mark/Ihor by EOD), Andries reviews the ~55 not-matched transactions and triggers missing-bill tasks for Marloes.
OI duplicate bank transactions fixed (NEO-1448, Mark, 6 Jul, in Bookkeeping bug-fixes); the durable fix is NEO-1299 (pull bank transactions from Exact and suppress already-booked ones).

Depreciation schedules and credit-note processing deprioritised for July (7 Jul) - moved back to Later on the board and Backlog in Linear; Andries had asked (6 Jul) for a depreciation schedule from w/c 7 Jul.
'Depreciation & accruals' split into two projects (6 Jul): 'Depreciation schedules' and 'Accruals'; requirements retagged accordingly.
End-to-end AR reconciliation remains the July commitment; it is currently unstaffed (David left 6 Jul).

risks.text: End-to-end AR reconciliation is the July commitment but is unstaffed since David left (6 Jul).
Full reporting requirements are not yet enumerated - scope could grow materially once DP's list lands.
~EUR 15K in outstanding receivables flagged with no active debtor management yet.
Andries asked for a depreciation schedule from w/c 7 Jul but depreciation was deprioritised for July - expectation to manage.

projects.name: End-to-end AR reconciliation
projects.summary: Match incoming payments to outbound invoices and close the open item in Exact; validated on seeded data first. Committed for July; currently unstaffed (David left 6 Jul). Needs a debtor-management view (~EUR 15K outstanding).

projects.name: Financial reporting
projects.summary: Reporting on the ledger; minimum set = trial balance, full GL export, P&L, balance sheet, aged receivables (30/60/90/120+), fixed-asset register, GL drill-down; VAT breakdown by rate/territory to Nextens. Full list pending from DP.

projects.name: Credit note processing (AR & AP)
projects.summary: Process credit notes both ways so reconciliation and the ledger reflect reversals/refunds; still to be specced. Deprioritised for July (7 Jul).

projects.name: Related-parties register
projects.summary: Register of related parties for intercompany payments; every related-party transaction carries a counterparty tag and GL drill-down shows the related party per line.

projects.name: Payroll (Numbrs integration)
projects.summary: Payroll via Numbrs; requirements not yet defined (not covered in the DP session).

projects.name: Third-party wallet transaction import (MVP)
projects.summary: Ingest third-party wallet/PSP transactions, Stripe first, so non-Swan banking customers feed transactions into neno.

projects.name: Depreciation schedules
projects.summary: Depreciation by asset-type group with a fixed-asset register (annual review for now). Split from 'Depreciation & accruals' on 6 Jul; deprioritised for July on 7 Jul.

projects.name: Native team features & membership permissions
projects.summary: neno-native team/member management and permissions, independent of Swan.

projects.name: Customer project creation & management
projects.summary: Customer-defined projects for project-based cost/revenue tracking; scope TBD.

projects.name: Accruals
projects.summary: Accrual and prepayment entries; approval-gated post-close adjustments. Split from 'Depreciation & accruals' on 6 Jul; not committed for July.

decisions.text: Reconciliation suggestions limited to one active suggestion per bill and per transaction (NEO-1451 shipped); the same constraint will likely be needed for AR reconciliation once it exists.
Workflow: once the 'Not matched' and 'Needs your review' lists are trustworthy, Andries reviews the ~55 not-matched transactions and triggers missing-bill tasks for Marloes.

Depreciation schedules and credit-note processing deprioritised for July - moved back to Later on the board and Backlog in Linear (Andries' 6 Jul depreciation-schedule request noted; deprioritised regardless).
'Depreciation & accruals' split into 'Depreciation schedules' (was committed, then deprioritised) and 'Accruals' (Backlog, not committed); requirements retagged.
OI duplicate bank transactions fixed (NEO-1448); durable fix is NEO-1299 (pull bank transactions from Exact and suppress already-booked ones).
Three of the next-5 cohort run payroll that currently needs a manual monthly CSV import from Numbrs; payroll-integration viability for Q3 to be determined.
Aged-receivables analysis (by customer, 30/60/90/120+ day buckets) is a minimum AR reporting requirement.
Q3 goal restated: 30 customers running on neno for bookkeeping, with neno as the single source of truth feeding Exact.
Intercompany interest auto-accrual deprioritised as a nice-to-have (currently tracked in Excel).
Depreciation review runs in July and annually for now; year-end balance correctness is the key requirement (monthly journals possible in Exact).

open_questions.text: Payroll design not yet discussed - remains an open design area for a future session.
Full reporting requirements list still being compiled by DP.
Feature sequencing and ownership for the post-GL bookkeeping scope is not yet decided.
Ocean Ionics Espana: separate entity or a second bank account? Booking treatment (intercompany vs investment) depends on the answer.
VAT filing period flexibility (monthly/quarterly/bi-monthly) must be configurable per customer and country.

next_steps.text: Make the 'Not matched' and 'Needs your review' lists trustworthy so Andries can trigger missing-bill tasks for Marloes.
Review the ~55 not-matched transactions and mark them receipt-missing / trigger tasks.
Compile the full reporting requirements list.
Define and file the remaining projects after end-to-end AR reconciliation.

meetings.title: Double-Entry Bookkeeping Open Questions
meetings.summary: Primarily a GL design session; produced the minimum reporting set and sharpened related-parties and depreciation/accruals requirements.
decisions: Minimum reporting set defined (trial balance, full GL export, P&L, balance sheet, aged receivables, fixed-asset register, GL drill-down)
Aged-receivables reporting (30/60/90/120+ buckets) confirmed as a minimum requirement
Intercompany interest auto-accrual deprioritised
open_questions: DP to compile full reporting requirements list
Ocean Ionics Espana: entity vs second bank account

meetings.title: Q2 Retro & Q3 Planning
meetings.summary: Quarter retro and Q3 planning across P&E, Services, Commercial and Finance; restated the 30-customer source-of-truth goal and the Q3 bookkeeping feature set.
decisions: Q3 goal: 30 customers running on neno with neno as the single source of truth feeding Exact
open_questions: Feature sequencing and ownership for the bookkeeping scope
next_steps: Define and file the remaining projects after AR reconciliation

ce07f00e-b392-40bd-85fb-2a4a831c9c80.sources_note: Slack #accounting-mvp (reconciliation-list trust + NEO-1451/NEO-1448 threads, 6-7 Jul) + Linear projects (Depreciation schedules / Accruals split, July deprioritisation) + the 23 Jun DP session; Granola folder had no new meetings in the lookback.
